raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 655 bans all sale and transportation of saltwater algae of the Caulerpa genus to reduce the chance of further invasions of coastal environments.",
    "Caulerpa prolifera was discovered in Newport Bay in 2021 and has yet to be fully eradicated.",
    "Caulerpa taxifolia invaded the Southern California coast in the early 2000s and cost millions to eradicate.",
    "The Caulerpa genus can rapidly outcompete California native plants in invaded areas, displacing wildlife and altering ecosystems.",
    "A single blade of Caulerpa can sprout a whole new plant, making removal difficult without constant monitoring.",
    "Caulerpa contains toxins that repel most fish outside its native range.",
    "Illegal dumping of aquarium water is the most likely source of the Caulerpa taxifolia invasion in the early 2000s and the current Caulerpa prolifera invasion.",
    "California banned the transportation and sale of Caulerpa taxifolia and several other Caulerpa species in 2001, but Caulerpa prolifera remained legal to sell and trade until Assembly Bill 655."
  ]
}
```
